Timed.description: Something planned or scheduled to be done at a particular time or lasting for a specified amount of time.

Unplanned.description: Something that has not been planned as part of the task.

Relation.description: Concerns the way in which two or more people or things are connected.

Comparative-relation.description: Something considered in comparison to something else.

Approximately-equal-to.description: (A (Approximately-equal-to B)) indicates that A and B have almost the same value. Here A and B could refer to sizes, orders, positions or other quantities.

Less-than.description: (A (Less-than B)) indicates that A is smaller than B. Here A and B could refer to sizes, orders, positions or other quantities.

Less-than-or-equal-to.description: (A (Less-than-or-equal-to B)) indicates that the relative size or order of A is smaller than or equal to B.

Greater-than.description: (A (Greater-than B)) indicates that the relative size or order of A is bigger than that of B.

Greater-than-or-equal-to.description: (A (Greater-than-or-equal-to B)) indicates that the relative size or order of A is bigger than or the same as that of B.

Equal-to.description: (A (Equal-to B)) indicates that the size or order of A is the same as that of B.

Not-equal-to.description: (A (Not-equal-to B)) indicates that the size or order of A is not the same as that of B.

Connective-relation.description: Indicates two items are related in some way.

Belongs-to.description: (A (Belongs-to B)) indicates that A is a member of B.

Connected-to.description: (A (Connected-to) B) indicates that A is related to B in some respect, usually through a direct link.

Contained-in.description: (A (Contained-in B)) indicates that A is completely inside of B.

Described-by.description: (A (Described-by B)) indicates that B provides information about A.

From-to.description: (A (From-to B)) indicates a directional relation from A to B. A is considered the source.

Group-of.description: (A (Group-of B)) indicates A is a group of items of type B.

Implied-by.description: (A (Implied-by B)) indicates B is suggested by A.

Interacts-with.description: (A (Interacts-with B)) indicates A and B interact, possibly reciprocally.

Member-of.description: (A (Member-of B)) indicates A is a member of group B.

Part-of.description: (A (Part-of B)) indicates A is a part of the whole B.

Performed-by.description: (A (Performed-by B)) Indicates that ction or procedure A was carried out by agent B.

Related-to.description: (A (Relative-to B)) indicates A is a part of the whole B.

Directional-relation.description: A relationship indicating direction of change.

Away-from.description: Go away from a place or object.

Towards.description: Moving in the direction of. A relation binding a relational quality or disposition to the relevant type of entity

Above.description: (A (Adjacent-to B)) means A is in a place or position that is higher than B.

Across-from.description: (A (Across-from B)) means A is on the opposite side of something from B.

Adjacent-to.description: (A (Adjacent-to B)) indicates that A is next to B in time or space.

Ahead-of.description: (A (Ahead-of B)) indicates that A is further forward in time or space in B.

Around.description: (A (Around B)) means A is in or near the present place or situation of B.

Behind.description: (A (Behind B)) means A is at or to the far side of B, typically so as to be hidden by it.

Below.description: (A (Below B)) means A is in a place or position that is lower than the position of B.

Between.description: (A (Between, (B, C))) means A is in the space or interval separating B and C.

Bilateral-to.description: (A (Bilateral B)) means A is on both sides of B or affects both sides of B.

Bottom-edge-of.description: (A (Bottom-edge-of B)) means A is on the bottom most part or or near the boundary of B.

Boundary-of.description: (A (Boundary-of B)) means A is on or part of the edge or boundary of B.

Center-of.description: (A (Center-of B)) means A is at a point or or in an area that is approximately central within B.

Close-to.description: (A (Close-to B)) means A is at a small distance from or is located near in space to B.

Far-from.description: (A (Far-from B)) means A is at a large distance from or is not located near in space to B.

In-front-of.description: (A (In-front-of B)) means A is in a position just ahead or at the front part of B, potentially partially blocking B from view.

Left-edge-of.description: (A (Left-edge-of B)) means A is located on the left side of B on or near the boundary of B.

Left-side-of.description: (A (Left-side-of B)) means A is located on the left side of B usually as part of B.

Lower-left-of.description: (A (Lower-left-of B)) means A is situated on the lower left part of B. This relation is often used to specify qualitative information about screen position.

Lower-right-of.description: (A (Lower-right-of B)) means A is situated on the lower right part of B. This relation is often used to specify qualitative information about screen position.

Outside-of.description: (A (Outside-of B)) means A is located in the space around but not including B.

Over.description: (A (over B)) means A above is above B so as to cover or protect or A extends over the a general area as from a from a vantage point.

Right-edge-of.description: (A (Right-edge-of B)) means A is located on the right side of B on or near the boundary of B.

Right-side-of.description: (A (Right-side-of B)) means A is located on the right side of B usually as part of B.

To-left-of.description: (A (To-left-of B)) means A is located on or directed toward the side to the west of B when B is facing north. This term is used when A is not part of B.

To-right-of.description: (A (To-right-of B)) means A is located on or directed toward the side to the east of B when B is facing north. This term is used when A is not part of B.

Top-edge-of.description: (A (Top-edge-of B)) means A is on the uppermost part or or near the boundary of B.

Top-of.description: (A (Top-of B)) means A is on the uppermost part, side, or surface of B.

Upper-left-of.description: (A (Upper-left-of B)) means A is situated on the upper left part of B. This relation is often used to specify qualitative information about screen position.

Upper-right-of.description: (A (Upper-right-of B)) means A is situated on the upper right part of B. This relation is often used to specify qualitative information about screen position.

Underneath.description: (A (Underneath B)) means A is situated directly below and may be concealed by B.

Within.description: (A (Within B)) means A is on the inside of or contained in B.

Temporal-relation.description: Any relationship which includes a temporal or time-based component.

After.description: (A After B) means A happens at a time subsequent to a reference time related to B.

Asynchronous-with.description: (A Asynchronous-with B) means A happens at times not occurring at the same time or having the same period or phase as B.

Before.description: (A Before B) means A happens at a time earlier in time or order than B.

During.description: (A During B) means A happens at some point in a given period of time in which B is ongoing.

Synchronous-with.description: (A Synchronous-with B) means A happens at occurs at the same time or rate as B.

Waiting-for.description: (A Waiting-for B) means A pauses for something to happen in B.

dB.description: Intensity expressed as ratio to a threshold. Often used for sound intensity.

candela.description: Units used to express light intensity.

cd.description: Units used to express light intensity.

currencyUnits.description: Units indicating the worth of something.

dateTimeClass.description: Date-times should conform to ISO8601 date-time format YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss. Any variation on the full form is allowed.

nameClass.description: Value class designating values that have the characteristics of node names. The allowed characters are alphanumeric, hyphen, and underbar.

numericClass.description: Value must be a valid numerical value.

textClass.description: Value class designating values that have the characteristics of text such as in descriptions.

allowedCharacter.description: A schema attribute of value classes specifying a special character that is allowed in expressing the value of a placeholder. Normally the allowed characters are listed individually. However, the word letters designates the upper and lower case alphabetic characters and the word digits designates the digits 0-9. The word blank designates the blank character.

defaultUnits.description: A schema attribute of unit classes specifying the default units to use if the placeholder has a unit class but the substituted value has no units.

extensionAllowed.description: A schema attribute indicating that users can add unlimited levels of child nodes under this tag. This tag is propagated to child nodes with the exception of the hashtag placeholders.

recommended.description: A schema attribute indicating that the event-level HED string should include this tag.

relatedTag.description: A schema attribute suggesting HED tags that are closely related to this tag. This attribute is used by tagging tools.

requireChild.description: A schema attribute indicating that one of the node elements descendants must be included when using this tag.

required.description: A schema attribute indicating that every event-level HED string should include this tag.

SIUnit.description: A schema attribute indicating that this unit element is an SI unit and can be modified by multiple and submultiple names. Note that some units such as byte are designated as SI units although they are not part of the standard.

SIUnitModifier.description: A schema attribute indicating that this SI unit modifier represents a multiple or submultiple of a base unit rather than a unit symbol.

SIUnitSymbolModifier.description: A schema attribute indicating that this SI unit modifier represents a multiple or submultiple of a unit symbol rather than a base symbol.

suggestedTag.description: A schema attribute that indicates another tag  that is often associated with this tag. This attribute is used by tagging tools to provide tagging suggestions.

tagGroup.description: A schema attribute indicating the tag can only appear inside a tag group.

takesValue.description: A schema attribute indicating the tag is a hashtag placeholder that is expected to be replaced with a user-defined value.

topLevelTagGroup.description: A schema attribute indicating that this tag (or its descendants) can only appear in a top-level tag group.

unique.description: A schema attribute indicating that only one of this tag or its descendants can be used  in the event-level HED string.

unitClass.description: A schema attribute specifying which unit class this value tag belongs to.

unitPrefix.description: A schema attribute applied specifically to unit elements to designate that the unit indicator is a prefix (e.g., dollar sign in the currency units).

unitSymbol.description: A schema attribute indicating this tag is an abbreviation or symbol representing a type of unit. Unit symbols represent both the singular and the plural and thus cannot be pluralized.

valueClass.description: A schema attribute specifying which value class this value tag belongs to.

boolProperty.description: Indicates that the schema attribute represents something that is either true or false and does not have a value. Attributes without this value are assumed to have string values.

unitClassProperty.description: Indicates that the schema attribute is meant to be applied to unit classes.

unitModifierProperty.description: Indicates that the schema attribute is meant to be applied to unit modifier classes.

unitProperty.description: Indicates that the schema attribute is meant to be applied to units within a unit class.

valueClassProperty.description: Indicates that the schema attribute is meant to be applied to value classes.

epilogue: This is an updated version of the schema format. The properties are now part of the schema. The schema attributes are designed to be checked in software rather than hard-coded. The schema attributes, themselves have properties.
